scores.anchor_matched: 0 anchor band: 0/10 canonical F1–F10 flows have an executable E2E test that drives the real UI and asserts chain state; P0 create-instance is only a skipped scaffold, and buy-via-sale/place-YES-bond have no specs.

evidence.note: F1 is explicitly described as a scaffold and implemented with test.fixme(), so it does not execute.
The intended viem chain assertions are commented out, so no post-action chain state is asserted.
The only active journey file is a read-only home smoke test, not one of the canonical F1–F10 flows.
The canonical inventory requires F1–F10 coverage; P0 flows include create-instance, buy-via-sale, create-proposal, and place-yes-bond.

scores.dimension: D2 — Test signal density (% non-useless tests)
scores.anchor_matched: 0 anchor band: the runnable tests are DOM/smoke-level and do not assert chain state or computed chain-dependent UI; the suite also has <=5 tests, so the hard cap would limit any score to 5.

evidence.note: The first runnable test checks page title, table visibility, focusability, and console/page errors, but no chain-backed postcondition.
The second runnable test checks filter button class toggles, a DOM-only assertion that does not depend on chain state.
The only paying-flow test is skipped with test.fixme(), so it contributes no useful signal.
Useful tests must assert chain state or chain-dependent computed UI; suites with <=5 tests are capped at 5.

scores.anchor_matched: 0 anchor band: the runnable tests do not touch a chain; Anvil fork, Synpress, and viem assertions are documented as intent but are not wired into executable tests.

evidence.note: The config says Synpress can be added later and, until then, only the read-only project runs.
The active tests are explicitly no-wallet/no-transaction read-only checks.
The wallet/fork test is gated on future data-testid, Synpress, and funded Anvil setup.
Synpress setup calls are commented out rather than executed.

scores.anchor_matched: 0 anchor band: no executable tests cover any of the 13 failure axes; listed failure cases are only comments in the skipped F1 scaffold.

evidence.note: Wallet rejection, wrong chain, RPC 500, Part 2 failure, and reorg are listed as later-pass failure coverage, not executable specs.
The F1 file uses test.fixme(), so even its happy path and commented failure plans do not run.
The rubric requires coverage across wallet, chain, RPC, tx, slippage, phase, account-switch, and reload failure axes.
The selector registry also marks future lint as not yet present, confirming failure-mode/chaos enforcement is not yet wired.

scores.anchor_matched: 3–5 anchor band: the scaffold defines selector conventions and active tests use some stable role selectors, but runnable tests still use brittle CSS/class assertions, the site is missing many required data-testid attributes, and there is no helpers/selectors.ts lint-enforced map.

evidence.note: Selector rules prefer data-testid/role and explicitly state that the source is currently missing many required testids.
One active test mixes a brittle CSS selector, table.rankings-table, with stable role-based selection for the connect button.
The filter test uses stable role selectors but asserts on the styling class filter-pill-active, which is brittle under the rubric.
The home page exposes class/data-rank-filter hooks but no data-testid for the rankings filter/table elements.
CI lint blocking missing testids is described as future work, not current enforcement.

scores.anchor_matched: 0–3 anchor band: a package script and config exist, but the documented command is not the rubric's npm test:e2e, the local runner failed in this checkout, no Anvil/Synpress fixtures exist, and CI does not invoke interface tests.

evidence.note: The repo has an e2e script and devDependencies for Playwright/Synpress/viem, but no npm test:e2e script or lockfile-backed install evidence.
The config points at tests-e2e and sets reporters/workers, but retries are 1 in CI rather than the required retries: 0 target.
Screenshots, traces, and video are configured on failure, which is positive local artifact scaffolding.
The CI workflow only runs Foundry fmt/build/tests; there is no e2e job or Playwright artifact upload.
The required complete-suite artifacts include test-e2e fixtures, helpers, CI e2e job, and Stryker config; these are not present in the current tree.
The command printed the package script header and then failed with 'error: unknown command test' because the local playwright binary on PATH is not @playwright/test in this checkout.

scores.anchor_matched: 3–5 anchor band: the research/rubric files define journeys and personas, and the skipped F1 spec has a clear flow header, but there is no full per-flow Gherkin/journey-map set, no machine check, and the active home spec is not linked to a canonical F1–F10 row.

evidence.note: The research dossier defines the desired Gherkin/journey format and an example flow scenario.
The dossier defines journey-map rows and a persona library requirement.
The F1 scaffold names the canonical flow, persona, happy path steps, and intended chain assertions.
The active smoke spec describes a browse/inspect persona but is not tied to a canonical journey-map row.
The 7/9 anchors require all flows mapped and every spec linked to a journey-map row, with CI machine checks at the top band.

evaluator_notes: No prior topic-2 evaluation line existed, so regression/improvement comparisons are null. The complete-suite aggregate cap of 6.5 is non-binding because the mean is already 1.7. Lower-anchor observations were not averaged away: D5 stays below 5 because the active tests still depend on CSS/class selectors despite some stable role selectors, and D1 remains in the 0 band because the only canonical paying-flow spec is skipped and has commented-out chain assertions.
